notes: Already in docs/phenotype_list.tsv. Range in cm. Classic highly polygenic trait -- SNP-h2 ~40-50% (Yengo et al. 2022), twin h2 ~80%. General-population literature estimate, not AoU-specific.
Already in docs/phenotype_list.tsv. Range in kg. SNP-h2 for weight alone is modest (~20-25%) -- bmi below captures the more commonly studied ratio trait. General-population estimate.

Already in docs/phenotype_list.tsv. Range in kg/m^2. SNP-h2 ~25-27% (Yengo et al. 2018 GIANT+UKB); twin h2 much higher (~40-70%) -- the gap is itself a well-studied phenomenon ("missing heritability"). General-population estimate.

Already in docs/phenotype_list.tsv. Range in mmHg. SNP-h2 ~15-20%, single-visit BP is noisy (no averaging across visits here) -- expect attenuated heritability relative to studies using multi-visit means. General-population estimate.
Already in docs/phenotype_list.tsv. Range in mmHg. Same single-visit-noise caveat as systolic_bp. General-population estimate.

Already in docs/phenotype_list.tsv. Range in mg/dL. pull_phenotype() doesn't distinguish fasting vs. random draws (see README) -- adds measurement noise, likely deflates apparent heritability relative to a fasting-restricted phenotype. General-population estimate.

Already in docs/phenotype_list.tsv. Range in % (NGSP). Integrates ~3 months of glycemic control -- less draw-timing-sensitive than glucose, generally a cleaner quantitative trait for that reason. General-population estimate.

Triglyceride [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma, LOINC 2571-8, confirmed via CDR query: n=198,673 persons. Range in mg/dL.

AoU's Physical Measurements module apparently doesn't populate this LOINC in practice, despite it being the semantically correct standard concept. NOT viable as a high-completeness phenotype -- recommend dropping waist_circumference/hip_circumference/waist_hip_ratio from the active panel unless a materially different source for waist circumference turns up. Range in cm.

COOL/AoU-SPECIAL. Not an OMOP measurement concept -- lives in AoU's heart_rate_summary wearables table, a different CDR schema entirely. source=='fitbit' is NOT wired up in pull_phenotype() (see 02_phenotype/README.md's 'Not yet filled in'). What's genuinely distinctive about AoU: longitudinal, near-continuous consumer-wearable data linked to WGS + EHR at a scale few public biobanks match. Real tradeoff against the 'few missing' goal -- only participants who owned/shared a Fitbit are represented (self-selected, a minority of the cohort). Worth pursuing for novelty, not completeness. SNP-h2 range from general wearable/accelerometer heart-rate studies, not AoU-specific.

COOL/AoU-SPECIAL. Lives in AoU's activity_summary wearables table. source=='fitbit' not wired up in pull_phenotype() -- same gap as fitbit_resting_heart_rate above. Physical activity is a comparatively under-studied complex trait; most large GWAS of activity levels (e.g. UK Biobank) use accelerometer data, which AoU's Fitbit cohort is a reasonable analog to. Same completeness caveat as fitbit_resting_heart_rate.

COOL/AoU-SPECIAL. Lives in AoU's sleep_daily_summary wearables table. source=='fitbit' not wired up in pull_phenotype() -- same gap as fitbit_resting_heart_rate above. Sleep duration/quality is a well-studied complex behavioral trait; AoU's continuous multi-year Fitbit sleep data is more detailed than most EHR-based or single-timepoint survey measures elsewhere. Same completeness caveat as fitbit_resting_heart_rate.

expected_completeness: VERY LOW (deprioritized -- depends on waist_circumference, N=22)
approx_snp_h2: ~0.2 (WHRadjBMI, a distinct and more commonly GWAS'd variant, has a well-established sex-heterogeneous signal -- see notes)
notes: DEPRIORITIZED: waist_circumference's confirmed concept has only 22 persons in the CDR -- this phenotype is very unlikely to be usable regardless of hip_circumference's resolution. NEW, DERIVED. Not a single measurement -- waist_circumference / hip_circumference for the same person, ideally same visit. concept_id holds both underlying concept_ids (waist,hip, in that order), matching waist_circumference/hip_circumference above once those are confirmed. source == 'derived_ratio' is NOT wired up in pull_phenotype() -- needs a new code path that pulls both concept_ids, joins on person_id (and nearest measurement_date, not just 'most recent value' independently for each, or the pairing could mix measurements from different visits), and computes the ratio; a materially different shape than every other row here. Body-fat-distribution trait, genetically partly distinct from BMI/overall adiposity -- WHR adjusted for BMI (WHRadjBMI) is the more standard GWAS phenotype in the literature (GIANT consortium) and shows notably stronger genetic signal in women than men; adjusting for BMI isn't implemented here (would mean using bmi as a regression covariate for another phenotype, not currently how pull_covariates()/build_covariate_sets() work) but is worth keeping in mind if this trait's results look unexpectedly weak or sex-inconsistent.

expected_completeness: medium (Lifestyle survey response, not universal like EHR labs)
notes: LIFESTYLE. AUDIT-C (Alcohol Use Disorders Identification Test - Concise), 3 items, fully included in AoU's Lifestyle survey and independently scorable per its own instructions (sum of 3 item responses, each 0-4, total 0-12) -- see https://support.researchallofus.org/hc/en-us/articles/6085586512660-Lifestyle-Baseline-Survey-Details-and-Resources . concept_id holds all 3 underlying question concept_ids (comma-separated) -- exact PPI/survey concept_ids not verified, check the AoU Data Browser (Survey domain, not Measurement) before use. source == 'survey_composite' is NOT wired up in pull_phenotype() -- needs a new code path pulling 3 survey answers per person from {CDR}.observation (not {CDR}.measurement) and summing them, similar in spirit to waist_hip_ratio's derived_ratio but summing instead of dividing, and reading PPI-coded survey responses (which need value_as_number OR a categorical-to-score mapping, depending on how AUDIT-C answers are stored -- confirm against the Data Browser before implementing). SNP-h2 estimate is for alcohol consumption/problematic-use phenotypes generally (e.g. Sanchez-Roige et al. 2019 PAU GWAS), general-population, not AoU-specific.

expected_completeness: low-medium (Lifestyle survey response, conditional on reporting current/former smoking -- smaller denominator than the survey's own response rate)
notes: LIFESTYLE. AoU's Lifestyle survey includes detailed tobacco-use items sourced from the Tobacco Use Supplement to the Current Population Survey (TUS-CPS) -- see https://support.researchallofus.org/hc/en-us/articles/6085586512660-Lifestyle-Baseline-Survey-Details-and-Resources . Only asked of/answered by participants who report current or former daily smoking, so completeness is meaningfully lower than the survey's overall response rate -- flagged explicitly since this cuts against the 'few missing' goal more than any EHR-sourced row above. concept_id not verified -- check the AoU Data Browser (Survey domain) before use. source == 'survey' is NOT wired up in pull_phenotype() (see phenotype_list.tsv's 'Not yet filled in'). SNP-h2 estimate is for smoking quantity phenotypes generally (e.g. GSCAN consortium's CigDay), general-population, not AoU-specific. Smoking pack-years (a more standard cumulative-exposure phenotype) would need this item combined with years-smoked and start/quit age -- a further derived_ratio/composite, not attempted here.
